---
title: "HW Class 6"
author: "Rowen (PID: A18363990)"
format: pdf
---

```{r}
library(bio3d)
```

## Q6: How would you generalize the original code above to work with any set of input protein structures?

This function takes a PDB ID, reads the protein, and plots the B-factor values from chain A alpha carbons. The plot shows which parts of the protein are more flexible and where drug binding could happen. The output of the function is a plot that shows B-factor values across the protein sequence.

```{r}
plot_protein_bfactor <- function(pdb_id) {
  
  pdb <- read.pdb(pdb_id)
  chainA <- trim.pdb(pdb, chain = "A", elety = "CA")
  plotb3(chainA$atom$b, sse = chainA, typ = "l", ylab = "B-factor")
}

```

```{r}
plot_protein_bfactor("4AKE")
```
